prs.title: [BUG]: fix HF tokenizer concurrent borrow in tool parsers
prs.author: yzong-rh
prs.body_head:
Co-authored with @sfeng33

## Purpose

Fix `RuntimeError: Already borrowed` that crashes concurrent tool-call requests when HuggingFace's Rust tokenizer backend is accessed simultaneously from multiple threads.

Fixes https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/34932

**Root cause:** Tool parsers are instantiated per-request, but their `__init__` methods calls a shared `tokenizer`. HuggingFace's fast tokenizer Rust backend panics on concurrent mutable borrows.

**Fix:**
In #35034, @sfeng33 [suggested](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35034#pullrequestreview-3957357477) encoding the static tokens eagerly at startup via a class method instead of the lazy-cache approach (`WeakKeyDictionary` + `Lock` + double-checked locking).

This PR implements that idea. Instead of caching tokenizer calls behind locks (the approach in #35034, #37169, #37382), the per-request code path simply cannot reach the tokenizer.

Introduce **`ToolParser.specialize(cls, tokenizer)`**, a classmethod that returns a dict of class attributes. Base `ToolParser` returns `{"vocab": ...}`. Subclasses override to add parser-specific fields (e.g. `bot_token_id`).

At startup, `ParserManager` calls **`maybe_specialize(parser_cls, tokenizer)`**, which then calls `parser_cls.specialize(...)` to get the specialized attributes and dynamically creates a subclass with those attributes baked in.

Per-request `ToolParser` instances then only reads ordinary class attributes -- no tokenizer access, no locking. 

**Fallback for tests** — if `ToolParser.__init__` detects direct construction without `maybe_specialize`, it specializes the class in-place with a warning. This preserves backwards compatibility for unit tests.

<details>
<summary>Files changed</summary>

| Category | Files | Change |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Base class | `tool_parsers/abstract_tool_parser.py` | `specialize()`, `_is_specialized` flag, `vocab` ClassVar, fallback in `__init__` |
| Unified parser ba

prs.title: [Build] Add Python 3.14 to supported version list.
prs.author: nascheme
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

This allows vLLM to built from source using Python 3.14.  This requires that the numba PR (#34764) is merged first.

Note that Python 3.13 would still be the recommended interpreter and that "ray" is not available yet for 3.14.

## Test Plan

Run builtin test suite with pytest.  E.g. `pytest src/vllm/tests`.

## Test Result

Most unit tests pass.  I have the following test failures:
```
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/distributed/test_ca_buffer_sharing.py - ValueError: Error initializing torch.distributed using env:// rendezvous: environment variable RANK expected, but not set
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/distributed/test_multiproc_executor.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/distributed/test_torchrun_example.py - ValueError: Error initializing torch.distributed using env:// rendezvous: environment variable RANK expected, but not set
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/distributed/test_torchrun_example_moe.py - ValueError: Error initializing torch.distributed using env:// rendezvous: environment variable RANK expected, but not set
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/entrypoints/openai/test_openai_schema.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/entrypoints/test_grpc_server.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/kernels/helion/test_utils.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/plugins_tests/test_stats_logger_plugins.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/utils_/test_mem_utils.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/v1/engine/test_engine_core_client.py - OSError: You are trying to access a gated repo.
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/v1/kv_connector/nixl_integration/test_edge_cases.py - ValueError: Please set the PREFILL_PORT, DECODE_PORT, and PROXY_PORT.
```
Looking at the CI scripting inside `./buildkite`, I gather these tests need either different hardware or some different pre-setup to pass.

prs.title: [Feature] TurboQuant: support hybrid models and uniform quantization
prs.author: JartX
prs.body_head:
# TurboQuant support for hybrid models

This PR fixes TurboQuant startup for hybrid models such as Qwen3.5, Qwen3-Next, and similar architectures.

Previously, TurboQuant would fail with a `NotImplementedError` as soon as it encountered Mamba layers. With this change, hybrid models are now handled correctly: TurboQuant is applied only to `full_attention` layers. 
## Additional fixes

While enabling proper hybrid support, this PR also fixes three additional issues:

- **Page-size planner mismatch:** The hybrid page-size planner was sizing attention pages using the standard formula, which does not match TurboQuant's packed K|V layout. As a result, every TurboQuant attention layer could trigger an assertion in the page merger. The planner now uses the TurboQuant-specific layout.

- **Incorrect backend selection for excluded layers:** If a layer was excluded from TurboQuant — for example because it was a skipped layer, a sliding-window layer, or a Mamba layer — the ROCm/CUDA backend selector could still incorrectly force the `TURBOQUANT` backend. These layers now correctly fall back to the default backend.

- **ROCm `flash_attn_varlen_func` incompatibility:** On ROCm, upstream `flash_attn_varlen_func` does not accept `out=`. A lightweight wrapper now detects that case and copies the result only when needed.

## Summary

Overall, this makes TurboQuant work reliably on hybrid architectures while preserving the current behavior and baselines for dense models.

prs.title: [ROCm] Add env flags to disable dynamic MXFP4 quant and enable AITER tuned GEMMs for Attention Projection Layers
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
For models in the DeepSeek family, dynamic MXFP4 quantization is enabled by default for the attention projection layers. This behavior may be suboptimal in certain deployment scenarios.

This PR introduces two opt-in environment variables that provide finer control over this path:

1. The ability to disable dynamic MXFP4 quantization for attention projections.
2. The ability to route unquantized attention GEMMs through AITER's newer FlyDSL-based tuned GEMM implementations, instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

In internal benchmarks, using the FlyDSL GEMM path for unquantized attention projections shows a good performance improvement on models such as Kimi-K2-Thinking.

## Summary
This PR adds two new ROCm environment variables to give operators finer control over quantization and GEMM dispatch for attention linear layers:
- **`VLLM_ROCM_DISABLE_ATTENTION_LINEAR_LAYER_DYNAMIC_MXFP4_QUANT`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, skips the dynamic MXFP4 quantization that is applied to attention projection layers (`q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`) which is done upon model load for a certain family of models.
- **`VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_TUNED_UNQUANTISED_GEMM`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, routes unquantized GEMMs in the attention path through AITER's tuned GEMM implementation (`aiter.tuned_gemm.tgemm.mm`) instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

Both flags are opt-in and default to the current behavior, so there is no change for existing users.

## Test Result
### Accuracy
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

Benchmark | Metric | Score | Threshold | Status
-- | -- | -- | -- | --
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (flexible-extract) | 0.944 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (strict-match) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS

### Performance
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

| config | conc | Baseline | AiterTunedFI |

prs.title: [ROCm] Implement GPU-to-NUMA-node detection
prs.author: pschlan-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Implement automatic NUMA topology detection on ROCm platforms via the amdsmi library (which is already a dependency).

## Test Plan
Run vLLM with `--numa-bind`, observe correct topology detection

## Test Result
Topology is detected correctly on the test system with 8 GPUs connected to 2 NUMA nodes:
```INFO 04-16 12:57:41 [numa_utils.py:111] Auto-detected NUMA nodes for GPUs: [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1]```

---

prs.title: Make v1 KV cache initialization messages device-neutral
prs.author: whk-lab
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Fix GPU-specific init-time KV cache reporting in `v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py` for shared initialization paths that are also used by non-GPU backends.

The KV cache memory accounting in this path already operates on generic `available_memory`, and CPU workers also feed into the same path via `determine_available_memory()`. However, several logs and errors still hardcode GPU-specific wording such as `GPU KV cache size` and `available GPU memory`, which is misleading on non-GPU backends.

## Test Plan
Ran the following locally:
```bash
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py -v
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py -k "report_kv_cache_config_uses_device_neutral_messages" -v
.venv/bin/pre-commit run --files vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py
```
## Test Result

- tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py: 2 passed
- tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py -k "report_kv_cache_config_uses_device_neutral_messages": 1 passed
- pre-commit on changed files: passed

On macOS arm64, uv pip install -r requirements/test.in could not be fully resolved because runai-model-streamer==0.15.7 does not provide compatible wheels for this platform, so I used a minimal local environment to run the targeted tests above.




---

prs.title: [ROCm] Add gfx1102/gfx1103 support
prs.author: mgehre-amd
prs.body_head:
gfx1103 (RDNA 3, e.g. Radeon 780M iGPU) was missing from the HIP_SUPPORTED_ARCHS list in CMakeLists.txt and from the compile-time architecture macros in skinny_gemms.cu and attention.cu.

The skinny GEMM and attention kernels defined __HIP__GFX1X__ and __HIP__GFX11__ by listing individual arch targets (__gfx1100__, __gfx1101__, __gfx1150__, __gfx1151__) but missed __gfx1103__. This caused wvSplitK kernel bodies to compile as UNREACHABLE_CODE (assert false) on gfx1103 while the wave32 reduction code was still active, resulting in a crash at runtime.

Replace __HIP__GFX11__ / __HIP__GFX12__ with the compiler-provided __GFX11__ / __GFX12__ umbrella macros which are defined for every target in each family. This avoids needing to enumerate individual targets and automatically covers future gfx11xx/gfx12xx additions.
